title: Werkstudent Portfolio & Property Management (m/w/d)
bonus_skills: Real estate specialization (Immobilienwirtschaft / Immobilienmanagement / Real Estate Management)
matched_skills: Business administration background — Master Management & Entrepreneurship (Leuphana) + Bachelor Management Administration (SCUT)
MS Office / Excel proficiency — listed tool; used for budget variance dashboards and burn-rate reports at HSBC
English C1 — strong written and spoken English evidenced across all roles
Structured and independent working style — demonstrated through solo PMO operations managing 6 workstreams at HSBC
IT and database affinity — Power BI PL-300, JIRA, Confluence; data storytelling and KPI framework design
Market research — ECE Group competitive intelligence reports; IPC performance analytics and ROI reporting
Data collection and analytical tool support — Power BI dashboards, KPI frameworks, internal reporting at HSBC and IPC
missing_skills: Very good German language (candidate holds B1 only; JD requires 'sehr gut', typically C1 level — this is a significant practical gap)
Real estate domain knowledge (no prior exposure to property management, leasing, or real estate transactions)
Portfolio analysis or real estate transaction preparation
core_responsibilities: General contract administration and processing of contract enquiries
Market research to support portfolio analyses and acquisition approaches
Processing rent, lease, and sublease increases including indexation clauses
Preparatory work for real estate transaction execution
Data collection to advance internal analysis tools
recommended_emphasis: Highlight Excel and Power BI skills explicitly — frame them as directly applicable to portfolio data collection and internal analysis tool development requested in the JD
Leverage the ECE Group role: market and competitive intelligence work maps well to the market research responsibility; make this connection explicit in the CV bullets
Reframe HSBC budget governance experience (USD 8M, burn-rate dashboards) to emphasize structured data management and financial analysis — transferable to portfolio tracking
Address the German language gap proactively — either note active improvement or (if applying) confirm current level and frame willingness to operate in German-language environment

Acknowledge the real estate domain gap but bridge it via transferable skills: structured analysis, vendor/contract coordination (IPC SOPs, ECE stakeholder coordination), and data-driven reporting — all relevant to property management support work
summary: Boru Lai is a strong structural match for this Werkstudent position at CBRE Germany: active Master's enrollment at Leuphana University satisfies the enrollment requirement, and the combination of business administration credentials, Power BI/Excel proficiency, structured PMO working style, and market research experience (ECE Group, IPC) aligns well with the data collection, market research, and analytical tool development duties listed in the JD. The primary gaps are the German language level (B1 vs. the required 'sehr gut') and the absence of any real estate domain background — the role explicitly prefers candidates with Immobilienwirtschaft specialization, and the core responsibilities around lease/contract management and transaction preparation have no direct analogue in the CV. Despite the real estate domain gap, the transferable analytical and coordination skills are solid, and the score of 84.2 reflects that most baseline competency requirements are met; however, the German language shortfall is a practical risk that should be disclosed or mitigated before applying.
